mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and model execution. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations to maximize performance.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.19.0.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs vLLM on your Kubernetes cluster using the Helm chart, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace vllm

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for vLLM.
> Installs vLLM in the vllm namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: vllm
spec:
  modelName: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    maxTokens: 100
EOF

```

> Creates a model resource named 'example-model' in the vllm namespace.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: vllm
spec:
  modelName: 'gpt-3'
  parameters:
    maxTokens: 100
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed model to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get models -n vllm

```

> Lists all models in the vllm namespace to verify the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes vLLM from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM release.
> Deletes the vllm namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade vLLM to a newer version, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get models -n vllm -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl get models -n vllm

```

> Backs up the current model configurations.
> Upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verifies that the models are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n vllm -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the vLLM service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the vLLM pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' and ensure all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model may not be deployed or the Custom Resource is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the model's existence with 'kubectl get models -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to run the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and consider scaling your cluster or adjusting resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall vLLM using the Helm install command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get models -n vllm' showing the status of your deployed models as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
